description: TCEL Tenant-9 AI Agent Ecosystem package (T9-007) - the agent-governance layer. In ECARMF the individual agents (BankRisk.AI, TradingRisk.AI, EnterpriseRisk.AI, CapitalAllocation.AI, Compliance.AI, ...) are declared by their own domain packages and auto-register in the kernel AgentRegistry; this package contributes the GOVERNANCE metadata around them: the agent-record and decision-council entities, the agent-lifecycle/permission event catalog, and AI-governance controls (deny high-risk tools without approval; route dissent to a human). Depends on Foundation. Omits ModelDriftDetected (Banking owns it); does NOT re-declare RecordReceived.
name: TCEL AI Agent Ecosystem

entities.description: Governance record for a registered AI agent (T9-007 ai-agent-record.schema.json): owner, validator, risk tier, lifecycle status.
A multi-agent decision-council record (T9-007 ai-decision-council.schema.json): participating agents, dissent, and the human decision required.

rules.name: High-risk tool requires approval
rules.description: Preventive/Critical (AI Governance). Deny an agent's high-risk tool request that lacks approval.
rules.reasonTemplate: AI-GOV-001: agent {agentId} high-risk tool request denied - no approval on file (AI Governance).

rules.name: Material dissent routes to a human
rules.description: Governance/Critical (CRO/AI Governance). A decision-council record with unresolved material dissent must route to a human, not auto-resolve.
rules.reasonTemplate: AI-GOV-002: decision council {councilId} has unresolved material dissent - routed for a human decision (CRO/AI Governance).

capabilities.name: TCEL AI Governance
capabilities.description: Agent registry governance, lifecycle, tool permissions, decision council, and AI incident handling for the TCEL tenant.
